pattern: Splitting yi + yj + |xi - xj| into (xi + yi already fixed) plus (yj - xj, the best partner seen within the last k units of x) turns the search into a sliding window whose running maximum can be kept with a monotonic deque.
hints: Because the points are already sorted by x, for a right point j the term |xi - xj| for any earlier i within k of xj is just xj - xi, so the whole expression splits into (xj + yj) plus (yi - xi) for some earlier i.

You only ever need the largest (yi - xi) among points whose x is within k of the current one -- everything further back can be discarded for good, since it will never be usable again as x keeps increasing.

Keep a deque of (x, y - x) pairs sorted by decreasing (y - x): drop expired entries off the front once they fall outside the k window, and before pushing a new point, pop any entries from the back whose (y - x) is no better than the new one, so the front is always the best usable partner.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two pairs both within k
statement example, only the first pair qualifies
k equal to zero with adjacent x values one apart is invalid, so use k covering the whole range
large k so every pair is eligible, favoring the extremes
best partner falls just outside the window and must be dropped
extreme coordinate values at the constraint boundary

stress.description: large strictly increasing x sequence with a guaranteed-eligible adjacent pair
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    xs = [rng.randint(-100000000, -1)]
    xs.append(xs[0] + 1)
    x = xs[-1]
    for _ in range(2, n):
        x += rng.randint(1, 1000)
        if x > 100000000:
            break
        xs.append(x)
    points = [[xs[i], rng.randint(-100000000, 100000000)] for i in range(len(xs))]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [points, k]
